required_skills: Spacecraft system design and subsystem integration across GNC, ADCS, power, thermal, and comms
Space environment effects modeling including radiation, atomic oxygen, UV, and thermal cycling
Spacecraft operations including mission phase planning, anomaly response, and command sequencing
Propulsion system integration for chemical and electric spacecraft propulsion
Electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) and RF interference (RFI) management for spacecraft
Spacecraft harness design and power distribution architecture
Mass properties management and center of mass (CoM) control for spacecraft configurations
Environmental testing execution per GEVS, ECSS, or MIL-STD-1540 qualification standards
Model-based systems engineering (MBSE) for spacecraft architecture definition and trade studies
Contamination control and cleanliness standards for optical and precision spacecraft payloads
preferred_skills: Experience with interplanetary or deep space mission design (Lagrange point, lunar, or planetary orbits)
Knowledge of optical payload integration including telescope alignment and image quality verification
Familiarity with in-space propulsion including electric propulsion (EP) system integration
Experience with space robotics or deployable mechanisms (solar arrays, antennas, booms)
Knowledge of NASA Technology Readiness Level (TRL) assessment and advancement methodology
Familiarity with autonomous on-board health management and fault protection systems
Experience with commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) component qualification for space applications
responsibilities: Lead spacecraft system architecture definition by decomposing mission requirements into subsystem specifications and managing interface control documents across all spacecraft subsystems
Perform spacecraft-level trade studies encompassing configuration, mass, power, thermal, and propulsion design choices to optimize mission performance within cost and schedule constraints
Manage spacecraft mass properties including center of mass and moment of inertia tracking across all design phases to ensure compatibility with launch vehicle and ADCS requirements
Coordinate spacecraft environmental qualification campaigns including vibration, acoustic, thermal vacuum (TVAC), and EMC testing to verify spacecraft readiness for the space environment
Develop and maintain spacecraft power system architecture including solar array sizing, battery depth-of-discharge budgets, and eclipse survival thermal heater power allocation
Define contamination control protocols and cleanliness verification procedures for sensitive optical payloads, precision mechanisms, and propulsion components
Integrate and verify spacecraft harness and electrical ground support equipment (EGSE) designs, ensuring signal integrity and electromagnetic compatibility across all spacecraft systems
Develop spacecraft operational concept (ConOps) documents and command and data handling procedures for all mission phases from launch through end-of-life
Support TRL advancement activities by planning and executing technology demonstration experiments to mature critical spacecraft technologies from TRL 4 to TRL 6
Conduct anomaly investigations during spacecraft integration and on-orbit operations by analyzing telemetry data, performing root cause analysis, and implementing corrective actions
